ACME WIDGETS INC. — FORM 10-K (FY2022)

Table of Contents

Item 1. Business   3

Item 1A. Risk Factors   9

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis   21

Item 7A. Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk   30

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data   34

Item 9. Changes and Disagreements   70

Item 1. Business

ACME Widgets Inc. designs and manufactures premium widgets and related accessories for industrial customers. The company operates three segments: Industrial Widgets, Consumer Widgets, and Widget Services. Founded in 1998, ACME sells through direct sales and a global distributor network across more than forty countries.

Our manufacturing facilities are located in three regions, and we rely on a diversified base of component suppliers. Competition is based on quality, price, reliability, and after-sales support.

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Our business faces significant competition risk from lower-cost manufacturers. A downturn in industrial demand could materially reduce our revenue. We also depend on a limited number of suppliers for key components, and supply chain disruptions could increase costs or delay shipments.

Cybersecurity incidents, foreign-exchange volatility, and changes in trade policy represent additional risks that could adversely affect our results of operations and financial condition.

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments

None.

Item 2. Properties

We lease our headquarters and own three manufacturing facilities.

Item 7. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue for fiscal 2022 grew 12% year over year, driven by strong demand in the Industrial Widgets segment and favorable pricing. Gross margin expanded by 130 basis points due to manufacturing efficiencies.

Operating expenses increased modestly as we invested in research and development. We expect continued investment in capacity during fiscal 2023. Liquidity remains strong with sufficient cash to fund operations.

Item 7A. Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk

We are exposed to interest-rate risk on our variable-rate borrowings and to foreign-currency risk on sales denominated in non-functional currencies. A hypothetical 100 basis point increase in interest rates would not have a material effect on our annual interest expense.

Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data

Net revenue for fiscal 2022 was 1,250 million dollars and net income was 180 million dollars. Total assets were 2,400 million dollars at year end. The accompanying notes are an integral part of these consolidated financial statements.

Item 9. Changes in and Disagreements with Accountants

None.
